summary: gate_evidence passes: current-head evidence is bound to the expected HEAD, stale reuse is not used as fresh, focused readiness remains summary-depth, and blocker next-command metadata is preserved across gate and LLM/readiness surfaces.

inspection.summary: Inspected review request, HEAD/git state, changed source/tests/spec/story docs, current PR artifacts, verification evidence, and focused readiness behavior. regression_guard: current typecheck/unit/e2e evidence is HEAD-bound to 7e3d67dfe7fb618a9229a00fa44d02c13e01c591 and the gate still blocks PR creation when unresolved gates remain. path_surface_coverage: checked --view readiness, --summary-json bounded projection coverage, full override tests, gate_status/execution_gate/unresolved_gates, LLM projection summarization, and PR evidence artifacts.
inputs: node bin/vibepro.js pr prepare . --story-id story-vibepro-gate-efficiency-fast-readiness --base origin/main --view readiness

judgment_delta: Evidence reuse was stale and focused views could hide Gate DAG blockers -> pass because stale reuse is not used as fresh, verification commands are current-head bound, and gate_status remains blocked until blockers with next commands are visible.

Summary-depth could silently drop needed output surfaces -> pass because evidence-plan records the summary override, still writes pr-prepare/evidence-plan/decision-index, and bounded projection preserves primary_next_command/next_commands.

CLI evidence-depth defaults and next-command enrichment could regress full PR paths -> pass because default/full override tests, focused workflow replay, typecheck, and full npm test passed on expected HEAD.
